narrative_claims.core_claim: Volcano travel is unpredictable, so JVTO focuses on operational certainty: disciplined decisions, clear safety boundaries, and realistic expectations.
mechanism: Risk-aware timing and route planning (night drives, steep hikes, long transfers).
Respect for official closures and safety instructions.
Plan B framework to keep the trip valuable when conditions change.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO runs private tours by default so timing, pacing, and safety decisions stay consistent for your group.
mechanism: Dedicated vehicle and crew for your booking (not mixed with other travellers).
Fewer compromises on timing, stops, and recovery time.
Simpler coordination for early starts and long transfers.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO uses an all-inclusive structure by default to reduce surprise costs and mid-trip negotiation.
mechanism: Clear inclusions/exclusions in writing (voucher is the booking reference).
Less on-road negotiation for tickets and logistics.
Payment steps and cancellation rules are centralized in policy pages.

narrative_claims.core_claim: On routes that include Mount Ijen, JVTO integrates health screening as a practical safety step, not a formality.
mechanism: Pre-hike checks aligned to access/safety controls (where required).
Clear guidance on what it is, why it matters, and what happens if a guest is not fit to climb.
Documentation flow is explained in the Travel Guide.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO organizes trust signals as a verification layer: clear claims, context, and evidence links — not just marketing statements.
mechanism: Proof Library pages map claim → context → evidence.
Partners and memberships are explained by function (competence, sustainability context, verification).
Press and recognition are listed with context, not just logos.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO treats reviews as a registry: grouped by platform and theme, so guests can validate patterns instead of reading random quotes.
mechanism: Platform-based browsing (Google, Trustpilot, etc.).
Theme tags (safety, punctuality, guiding quality, logistics).
Link from reviews to crew profiles and common routes.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO’s product is executed by real people. The Team Registry shows roles, routes, and proof from review mentions — not generic bios.
mechanism: Crew profiles focus on role, strengths, languages, and common routes.
Each profile links to reviews that mention the crew member.
Operational policies are kept on Standards/Policy pages to avoid duplication.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO partnerships are explained by function: competence context (HPWKI), sustainability context (INDECON), and verification logic (ISIC).
mechanism: Each partner is described with: what it is, why it matters to guests, and how it is used (not just a logo).
Partner references are organized in the Proof Library.
Partner claims avoid overstatement; they provide context rather than guarantees.

narrative_claims.core_claim: JVTO lists press and recognition with context so guests can evaluate third‑party references, not just branding claims.
mechanism: Each mention is listed with what it supports (context), not as a blanket endorsement.
Where possible, include bibliographic or publication metadata to reduce ambiguity.
Press/recognition lives in Proof Library and links back to claims it supports.

destinations.name: Mount Bromo
destinations.summary: Mount Bromo is an active volcano within the Tengger Caldera, famed for its sunrise views from Kingkong Hill, the Sea of Sand jeep adventure, and the Tenggerese Hindu rituals at Pura Luhur Poten.
destinations.highlight: Sunrise over Tengger Caldera and sacred Hindu ceremonies
destinations.seo_title: Mount Bromo Sunrise Tour - Complete Tengger Caldera Guide
destinations.seo_description: Experience Mount Bromo sunrise, Sea of Sand adventure, and Tenggerese Hindu culture. Complete travel guide to East Java's most iconic volcano. JVTO private tours include Jeep transfers and cultural insights.

destinations.name: Ijen Crater
destinations.summary: Kawah Ijen is the world's largest acidic crater lake and one of only two places where natural blue fire appears at night.
destinations.highlight: Blue fire phenomenon and acidic crater lake
destinations.seo_title: Mount Ijen Blue Fire Volcano - Complete Trekking Guide 2024
destinations.seo_description: Experience Mount Ijen's blue fire and acidic crater lake. Complete guide to trekking, safety, permits, and cultural significance in East Java.

destinations.name: Madakaripura Waterfall
destinations.summary: A sacred 200-meter canyon waterfall believed to be the final meditation site of Gajah Mada, symbolizing loyalty and unity from the Majapahit legacy.
destinations.highlight: Sacred 200-meter canyon waterfall and Gajah Mada's meditation site
destinations.seo_title: Madakaripura Waterfall - Gajah Mada's Sacred Sanctuary
destinations.seo_description: Discover Madakaripura, the sacred 200-meter waterfall where Gajah Mada meditated. Complete guide to visiting, cultural significance, and Majapahit history.

destinations.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
destinations.summary: A multi-stream semicircular waterfall formed by Mount Semeru's volcanic erosion, known for its canyon trail and panoramic viewpoint with Semeru backdrop.
destinations.highlight: Thousand-stream waterfall curtain with Mount Semeru backdrop
destinations.seo_title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall - Java's Most Spectacular Water Curtain
destinations.seo_description: Experience Tumpak Sewu, the thousand-stream waterfall with Mount Semeru backdrop. Complete adventure guide to the canyon descent and photography.

destinations.name: Papuma Beach
destinations.summary: Beautiful white sand beach with unique rock formations and clear turquoise waters in Jember, East Java
destinations.highlight: White sand beach with turquoise waters and unique rock formations
destinations.seo_title: Papuma Beach Jember - White Sand Paradise in East Java
destinations.seo_description: Discover Papuma Beach, Jember's white sand paradise with turquoise waters and unique rock formations.

We did the 3D2N Bromo and Mount Ijen tour. It was organised very well and our tourguide Rendi as well as our driver Holili were super kind and helpful! Great experience :) the accommodations were very nice as well.
Great guide and very efficient
Our tour guide (KiKi) and driver (Nur) was amazing. Communication was easy, instructions were clear, and they made the experience fun and safe. Kiki also took amazing photos for us. 100% recommend

Son geniales
Súper contentos de nuestros guías taufik y holili. Nos han ayudado en todo momento y nos han acompañado en todo. Son geniales y nos hemos reído mucho con ellos. Os aconsejamos a esta pareja de guías para vuestro viaje . Hacen que te sientas muy bien .
Os esperamos en España taufik y holili 😉

We have such a wonderful moments
Even in the bad weather ,anyway we can enjoy the trip. All The guides are very kindness, friendly and sincerely especially Yandi and Rendi , two thumbs up!!!!!!

It was a very fun trip
It was a very fun trip. The driver, Yandi, took great care of us the whole time and brought us everywhere we wanted to go. The guides, Rendi and Yandi, communicated well in English and helped take really great photos. If you’re planning to travel to Surabaya, I highly recommend this place.

Rendi&yandi good guide
Rendi&yandi good guide

Ahboy was an amazing guide.
Ahboy was an amazing guide- knowledgeable, caring and fun. Wr're so glad he showed us ljen.

Amazing trip
Had an amazing trip visiting Bromo, Ijen Crater, and Tumpak Sewu Waterfall! Everything was well organized and the price was really good. The staff were super kind and helpful — special thanks to Yandi (our driver who’s been staying with us the whole trip) and Boy (our guide to Ijen crater). They were so friendly and shared lots of useful info. Even though we had to wake up in the middle of the night, it was totally worth it! Highly recommended
The tour guides were extremely friendly and accommodating. One of our friends was injured and they helped him as well. Overall, fantastic planning from the team!

What an unbelievable experience I don't think there is a better tour guide anywhere than Anjas the personal touch he provides and the knowledge he as over everywhere we went will put this company head and shoulders above the rest.

JVTO takes pride in assuring their customers by giving them prompt prior information and were quick to answer any questions we had. The driver Yandi and guide Boy, who were assigned to us, were great.
Rendi and Fredi were great tour hosts. All your needs were taken care of throughout the tour. All the days were fully explained in advance what to expect and when to be ready.

We recently completed a tour by Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO) to Mt Bromo as well as IJEN and we were very satisfied with the arrangements made by the team. The team was very responsive, the trip was organised smoothly and our driver, Fredi, did a fantastic job, ensuring that we got from place to place safely and on time. We would certainly recommend going for a tour with JVTO!

We joined the 3D2N Mount Bromo, Madakaripura Waterfall, and Ijen Crater Tour and had an awesome time!

The tour was really well organized, with hikes spaced out just right so we had enough rest. There was also plenty of time to check out each spot. The food and accommodation were way better than we expected.

Big thanks to our guide Gufron—he's super friendly and fun to hang out with. His briefings (and pretty drawing) before each hike were professional, and he looked after us the whole trip. He also knows all the best spots for photos! Our driver Fredi was always on time. The whole crew made the trip amazing and totally unforgettable.

We highly recommend this tour!
10/10!!
Ahboy was a phenomenal Ijen guide from start to end. Not only was he incredibly knowledgeable and went out of his way to make sure everything we needed was sorted out (safety, logistics, equipment).
Visiting Mount Ijen was such a beautiful experience. We had a great time with our guide Ahboy he was so fun and helpful!

Incredible experience! We did 4D3N and they delivered on everything that was promised. We found this tour company very honest compared to others because you know the hotels that you'll go to (which are great btw).\nOur driver, Yandi, was always very kind and attentive. He never rushed us, letting us take our time to rest, while always staying on schedule. He also brought us to have lunch and dinner in very nice restaurants with very reasonable prices. And he even secured us some great spots to see the sunrise in Bromo. Most importantly, mainly thanks to him, we were literally one of the first to reach Tumpak Sewu, Bromo and Ijen, so we could enjoy them without big crowds. Big thanks to Anjas too for being a super nice guide and helping us a lot in Ijen!\nYou should really consider this tour company if you wanna visit these amazing natural treasures in East Java!

It was the best experience ever!! Our driver Pras and guide Rendi were the best, great people, super professionals and thoughtful, they took care of every detail and made us feel super safe and good.\nI recommended it 100%

(Translated by Google) I was delighted to participate in the meticulously planned 9-day, 8-night itinerary by JVTO Java Volcano Tours. From the initial itinerary consultation, to the professional guide's explanations, to the impeccable transportation and accommodation arrangements, to the breathtaking volcanic landscapes, every detail showcased the team's dedication and professionalism.
This trip left us with unforgettable and wonderful memories.

(Original)
非常開心參加由 JVTO爪哇火山旅遊公司為我們精心規劃的行程。這趟旅程是9天8夜的客製化行程。由最初的行程溝通，到專業的導覽解說、再來完善的交通、住宿安排，到震撼人心的火山景觀，每一個細節都展現出團隊的用心與專業。
這次旅程留下難以忘懷的美好回憶。
I recently joined a tour to East Java with JVTO, and it was absolutely incredible! Our guide, Gufron, and driver, Holili, made the entire trip so comfortable and safe.
Being a solo traveler it was safe and stress free experience with JVTO. The staffs were on time and friendly. Loved the hotels and food recommendations got from the guides.
Me and my girlfriend had an amazing trip over here. We did the 3D2N Ijen + Bromo tour and everything was well arranged. Special thanks to the guide Gufron, who made this trip special ❤️

(Translated by Google) We did the 3D/2N Bromo-ijen-Madakaripura tour with them, and the experience couldn't have been better.

We looked at several options and decided on this one because the accommodations were better and included more meals, for a super reasonable price. As for the tour, they all offer practically the same things...

I'm giving them 5 stars because I can't give them more.

The organization of the entire tour is excellent, even though we booked with very little notice. Even before leaving Spain, the contact has been continuous, and from the minute we set foot in the airport, they have been attentive to us (and our belongings) at all times.

The tour is intense, but it's so worth it. I hope to keep it in my memory forever.
Transfers to the airport, train, etc. Meals, accommodation, guide, driver, tickets...
It's perfectly designed so that all you have to worry about is enjoying it.

Our guide, Ahboy, made a tremendous effort with us since we barely spoke English, but he made sure we understood each other perfectly. He was attentive, helpful, funny, a top-notch photographer, and a true professional.

And our driver, Holili, made sure we were the first to arrive wherever we went, driving carefully and with a constant smile on his face.

Thank you so much to the whole team!

The experience was truly a 10!

(Original)
Hicimos con ellos el tour de 3D/2N de Bromo-ijen-Madakaripura, y la experiencia no ha podido ser mejor.

Miramos bastantes opciones, y nos decidimos por esta porque los alojamientos eran mejores e incluía más comidas, por un precio súper razonable, en cuanto al recorrido todas ofrecen prácticamente lo mismo…

La organización de todo el tour es buenísima, y eso que nosotros contratamos con muy poquito tiempo de antelación, pero desde antes de salir de Espańa el contacto ha sido continuo, y desde el minuto 1 que pusimos un pie en el aeropuerto han estado pendientes de nosotros (y nuestras pertenencias) en todo momento.

El Tour es  intenso, pero merece muchísimo la pena. Espero tenerlo en mi retina para siempre

(Translated by Google) I participated in a tour of Java's Mount Ijen Volcano, Mount Bromo, and Madakaripura Waterfall. The comprehensive outdoor plan included airport transfers, meals, accommodation, and a guide. Our accommodation, Riverside Homestay, even had a pool, making for a truly enjoyable stay. The tour features a highly efficient and reliable system, with the driver and guide contacting each other in advance and accompanied by photos. Both our driver, Freddy, and the mountain guide boy were cheerful and helpful, helping with photography and accommodating even the smallest of requests. Each of the three tourist sites was truly worthy of being called the main event. With some motorbike travel and river fording, the tour was packed with extraordinary experiences. There were also opportunities to sample local fruit, and the opportunity to ride a horse for just 3,000 yen—exceptional experiences unimaginable in Japan. The only thing I noticed was that the accommodation on the second day was a little noisy. Furthermore, the roads are narrow in all locations, and Mount Bromo in particular is filled with jeeps, making it difficult for beginners to stop or make U-turns. For this reason, we recommend hiring a guide. Considering accommodation, meals, transportation, photography assistance, etc., the service is exceptionally good for this price, so we highly recommend it.
In particular, the driving involved a six-hour round trip from the airport, as well as nighttime transportation and guiding, so we were grateful to Freddie, who accompanied us with a smile until the very end despite the hard work.

(Original)
ジャワ島のイジェン火山、ブロモ山、マダカリプラの滝をめぐるツアーに参加しました。空港からの送迎、食事、宿泊、ガイドの総合的なアウトドアプランに加え、宿泊先のリバーサイドホームステイはプールもあり、快適な時間を過ごすことができました。ツアーではあらかじめ写真付きの運転手とガイドの連絡があり、完成度が高い安心できる仕組みが採用されています。運転手のフレディも山岳ガイドのボーイも明るく親切で、写真撮影への協力や、ちょっとした要望にも応えていただくことができました。３つの観光地は全てがメインイベントというに相応しいレベルの内容です。バイクでの移動が一部あったり、川の徒渉があったり、非日常感は満載です。
現地の果実が食べれるタイミングがあったり、たった３０００円程度で馬に乗れたり、日本では考えられない破格の体験も可能です。

特に運転については６時間の空港付近からの往復に加え、夜間からの送迎やガイドもあるので、重労働にもかかわらず最後まで笑顔で付き合ってくれたフレディには感謝しています。

We had an incredible journey with JVTO during our recent trip to Tempak Sewu Waterfall and Mount Bromo! From the moment we embarked, everything was meticulously planned and executed flawlessly. Overall, our experience with JVTO was outstanding, and we wholeheartedly recommend their services to fellow travelers. We're already looking forward to booking another tour with JVTO in the future, particularly to visit Mount Ijen once conditions allow.

Trip of a Lifetime: East Java Adventure

Our recent journey through East Java with JVTO was nothing short of extraordinary. We took the 5D4N package and over the course of several days, we explored some of the region's most iconic destinations, including Tumpak Sewu Waterfall, Mount Bromo, and Mount Ijen.

Overall Experience:
Everything is prepared and well-planned for the entire trip. All detailed information is written on the website and you will easily have a worry - free journey.
Their passion for East Java was infectious, and their attention to detail ensured that every aspect of our trip was smooth and enjoyable. From arranging comfortable accommodations to recommending local delicacies, JVTO went above and beyond to make this trip unforgettable.

Conclusion:
We cannot recommend JVTO highly enough for anyone planning to explore East Java. Their expertise, professionalism, and genuine enthusiasm for sharing the wonders of their homeland made our journey an absolute delight.

Thank you, Freddy and Juan , for an incredible adventure that exceeded all expectations.
We look forward to our next exploration with you!

Final Thoughts:
If you're looking for a memorable tour experience that goes beyond the typical sights, JVTO is the perfect choice.
Thank you, JVTO for an unforgettable day!
Really great tour with an amazing guide (Kiki) who went out of his way to make sure we had an amazing experience. Beautiful sights!

faqs.question: How can we contact JVTO before and during the tour?
faqs.answer:
Before and during your tour, our official channels are:

  • WhatsApp (primary)
  • Email

These details appear on your E-Voucher and on the Contact page. Please make sure your phone number (including country code) and email address are correct so we can reach you with any important updates.

faqs.question: Which documents should I keep with me?
faqs.answer:
We recommend keeping the following easily accessible:

  • Passport and required visas
  • Travel insurance information
  • Your JVTO E-Voucher and contact details (printed or on your phone)
  • Any relevant medical information you may need to show in an emergency

faqs.question: Where can I read the full legal policy?
faqs.answer:
The full legal text of our booking and cancellation terms is available on:

  • Booking Information (web page summary)
  • Booking & Cancellation Policy (document)

In any confusion, the combination of your JVTO E-Voucher, the Booking Information page and the official policy document is treated as the final reference.

faqs.question: How much luggage can I bring?
faqs.answer:
In most cases, standard airline-style luggage is fine:

  • One main suitcase or backpack per person
  • One small daypack

If you plan to travel with unusually large or heavy items, please tell us in advance so we can plan the right vehicle. On days with hikes, we recommend leaving your main luggage in the car or accommodation and carrying only what you need in a daypack.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for these tours?
faqs.answer:
The key is layering, grip and simplicity:

  • Layered clothing for cold pre-dawn starts and warmer later hours
  • Closed shoes with good grip (hiking shoes recommended for Ijen and Tumpak Sewu)
  • Small backpack, personal headlamp or torch, waterproof cover for electronics
  • Essential personal medication

We recommend keeping non-essential valuables and jewellery to a minimum, and avoiding silver items during Ijen days due to sulphur. For full details, see Packing & Fitness.

faqs.question: How does the Ijen health screening work?
faqs.answer:
For tours that include the Ijen night hike, JVTO includes a mandatory health screening run by approved medical staff. The aim is to reduce the risk of sending unfit guests into a demanding environment with sulphur gas and steep trails.

Key points:

  • Screening takes place before the hike, often at your accommodation or a partner clinic.
  • Basic checks are done (for example blood pressure, oxygen saturation and relevant medical history).
  • Results are recorded and can be used to confirm whether the hike is appropriate.
  • A digital system with QR verification is used to help prevent fake certificates.

Full details, including what happens if you are not cleared, are explained on Ijen Health Screening.

faqs.question: What happens if I do not pass the Ijen health screening?
faqs.answer:
If screening staff or our team advise that you should not attempt the hike, we will follow that decision. It is made to protect your safety and others on the trail.

In these cases, we may:

  • Offer alternative low-risk activities or rest time, where practical
  • Continue the rest of your itinerary as planned

Financial treatment (for example how costs for the Ijen segment are handled) follows the rules set in our Booking Information and policy. Please read that page carefully before you book.

faqs.question: Do I need travel insurance?
faqs.answer:
Yes, we strongly recommend that all guests hold valid travel insurance that covers:

  • Medical emergencies
  • Evacuation and repatriation
  • Trip interruption and cancellations

JVTO operates as a licensed Indonesian tour operator, but we cannot act as an insurance provider and cannot cover costs that fall under personal insurance.

faqs.question: Can children or older travellers join JVTO tours?
faqs.answer:
Many families and older travellers have joined our tours safely and comfortably. The key is matching itinerary difficulty to the real fitness and health of the guests:

  • Some routes (for example Tumpak Sewu and the full Ijen hike) are physically demanding.
  • Others can be adapted with more viewpoints and less intense trails.

Please tell us clearly about ages and relevant conditions when you first contact us. We may suggest a slower or gentler plan based on your group’s needs.

faqs.question: What if I feel unwell during the tour?
faqs.answer:
Tell your driver or guide as soon as you notice it – do not wait until it becomes an emergency. Because your tour is private, we have more flexibility to:

  • Adjust the pace
  • Add extra rest stops
  • Change or skip certain activities when it is safer to do so

For serious symptoms, we will prioritise getting you to appropriate medical support. Always follow the instructions of your crew in such situations.

faqs.question: Do you offer ISIC student deals?
faqs.answer:
Yes. Through our collaboration with ISIC, verified international students may access adapted rates on selected packages. The goal is to keep travel fair for students facing higher foreign entrance fees, without reducing safety or quality.

The exact conditions and eligible tours are explained on our ISIC / Student Deals page.

faqs.question: Can you arrange an official police escort for our group?
faqs.answer:
For large groups (typically around 18 guests or more), JVTO can help coordinate an official traffic police escort on certain road segments (for example from a toll exit to Bondowoso), if approved by the relevant Traffic Police unit.

Key points:

  • Escorts are based on formal written orders from the authorities
  • Routes and times are clearly defined
  • The aim is safe convoy management, not speeding or bypassing laws

This is not automatic and not a marketing gimmick. Full details are on Police Escort for Groups.

faqs.question: Are your tours private or shared?
faqs.answer:
All JVTO tours are private only. We do not mix your booking with other guests or sell “seat in coach” options. Your vehicle, driver and guide are arranged for your party alone.

For examples of private itineraries from Surabaya or Bali, see our Tours pages.

faqs.question: What is included in the price?
faqs.answer:
Each tour page shows a clear list of Inclusions and Exclusions. In general, our private, all-inclusive packages cover:

  • Private vehicle and driver
  • Bromo 4WD jeep where listed
  • Stated park entrance tickets and permits
  • All hotel breakfasts
  • Selected lunches and dinners (especially during the Bondowoso / Ijen segment)
  • Mineral water
  • Essential gear for Ijen (gas mask and trekking pole) on tours that include Ijen
  • JVTO travel T-shirt (where listed)

Anything not listed under “Included” is treated as not included. For full details, please see Booking Information and the Inclusions & Exclusions section of your chosen tour.

faqs.question: How do I book and confirm a tour?
faqs.answer:
The usual steps are:

  1. You send us your dates, origin (for example Surabaya or Bali), group size and preferred route.
  2. We send you a tailored proposal with itinerary and price.
  3. You confirm in writing and pay the booking deposit (normally 20% of the total).
  4. We send you an official JVTO E-Voucher with your booking details.

Your booking is considered confirmed when the deposit has been received and the E-Voucher has been issued. Full details are on the Booking Information page.

faqs.question: How do payments, deposits and deadlines work?
faqs.answer:
In most cases:

  • A deposit is required to secure your booking.
  • The remaining balance is due a few days before Day 1 of your tour, depending on the payment method (for example card vs bank transfer).
  • If payment deadlines are missed, your booking may be at risk.

The exact amounts and dates are always stated in your E-Voucher and explained on our Booking Information page. That page and your E-Voucher together act as the official reference.

faqs.question: What is Travel Credit and do you offer cash refunds?
faqs.answer:
JVTO uses a Travel Credit system instead of standard cash refunds for most guest-initiated cancellations. In general:

  • If you cancel far enough in advance (before the stated cutoff), we convert your paid amounts into Travel Credit.
  • Travel Credit can be used for future JVTO tours, and may be transferable under the rules stated in our policy.
  • Travel Credit does not normally expire.
  • Cash refunds are not normally provided except in specially defined situations.

Exact timing thresholds, percentages and special cases are described in detail on the Booking Information page and in the Booking & Cancellation Policy.

faqs.question: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my dates?
faqs.answer:
We follow official instructions from Indonesian authorities and park management. Depending on the situation:

  • We may adjust timings or use alternative viewpoints
  • We may replace certain stops with other available local visits
  • In more serious cases, specific segments (such as a night hike) may be cancelled while other parts of the tour continue

How this affects your booking financially is explained in the Weather & Closures and Booking Information pages. External closures are handled differently from voluntary cancellations.

  • We may adjust timings or use alternative viewpoints

faqs.question: Can you guarantee sunrise views or blue fire?
faqs.answer:
No. Weather, cloud, volcanic gas and other natural factors are outside our control.

  • We design our itineraries to maximise the chance of good conditions
  • We cannot promise specific colours, light or blue fire visibility on any given night

Our commitment is to honest briefings and to acting quickly if conditions become unsafe.

faqs.question: If it rains, will our tour be cancelled?
faqs.answer:
Light or normal rain rarely cancels an entire tour, but it can:

  • Make trails more slippery
  • Reduce visibility
  • Require changes in timing or route

Severe weather, landslides or dangerous conditions can lead to partial or full cancellations for safety. Our Weather & Closures page explains how we respond and how this links to Travel Credit and itinerary changes.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to Cemoro Lawang
itinerary_days.activity: Your adventure begins with a pickup from Surabaya International Airport, any hotel in Surabaya City, or Surabaya Train Station (before 12 PM). Enjoy a scenic 3-4 hour drive to Cemoro Lawang, with a stop en route at a local restaurant for lunch (own expense). Upon arrival at Cemoro Lawang, check in to your hotel and take a rest. The rest of the evening is free for you to relax and enjoy the cool mountain air.

itinerary_days.title: Stargazing & Sunrise at Mount Bromo
itinerary_days.activity: Begin the day early at 2:00 AM for a stargazing experience under Mount Bromo’s clear skies. Witness the Milky Way and a spectacular sunrise over the Bromo landscape. Marvel at Mount Batok’s dormant core, Mount Bromo’s active crater, and Mount Semeru’s majestic peak in the distance. Capture the breathtaking vistas of Java’s volcanic terrain.After sunrise, travel across the Sand Sea on a 4WD or motorcycle to reach Poten, a Tenggerese Hindu temple. From there, you can either hike or take a horse ride (3 km) to the base of Mount Bromo. Climb 253 steps to the crater rim for an up-close view of its active core. After exploring, return to your hotel in Cemoro Lawang for breakfast and relaxation. The rest of the day is free for you to unwind or explore the area at your leisure.

itinerary_days.title: Madakaripura Waterfall & Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity: After breakfast at the hotel, check out at 10 AM and drive 1 hour to Madakaripura Waterfall. Upon arrival, switch to a motorbike or walk with a local guide for a 30-minute trek to the waterfall. Be prepared with comfortable sandals to navigate the slippery terrain as you enjoy the lush surroundings and cascading waters.After exploring the waterfall, continue your journey back to Surabaya. The 4-hour drive concludes at your preferred drop-off location, whether at a hotel, airport, or train station. Please ensure your flight or train departure is after 7 PM to allow ample time for the return journey. This marks the end of your unforgettable Bromo and Madakaripura trekking experience.

itinerary_days.title: Arrival in Surabaya & Transfer to Lumajang City
itinerary_days.activity:
Welcome to Surabaya & Journey to Malang
Your adventure begins with a pick-up from Surabaya Airport (preferably before 12:00 PM) and a scenic 3-hour drive to Lumajang City. Along the way, we will stop at a local restaurant for lunch (at your own expense). Upon arrival in Lumajang, check into your hotel, relax, and rest to prepare for the exciting days ahead.

itinerary_days.title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall & Journey to Bromo
itinerary_days.activity:
Tumpak Sewu Waterfall Adventure
Start the day with breakfast at the hotel, and then start to exploring Tumpak Sewu Waterfall. Known as the most beautiful waterfall in Java, Tumpak Sewu features a unique curtain-like water flow formed by multiple mini waterfalls cascading over a 300-meter-high cliff. The waterfalls form a semi-circular shape, and local legend has it that the waterfall is called "Sewu," meaning "one-thousand," due to the number of mini falls.
#next-stop#
Trekking to Tumpak Sewu & Water Spa
To fully appreciate the beauty of Tumpak Sewu, you will follow a trekking path up and over the rocks towards the top of the ravine, crossing the river at one point to reach Coban Sewu Waterfall. It’s recommended to wear proper trekking shoes or sandals. After spending time at the waterfall and enjoying the natural water spa, you’ll have lunch before continuing the journey to your hotel in the Bromo area, which takes approximately 4 hours by car.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise & Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity:
Bromo Sunrise & Stargazing Adventure
On this final day, prepare for an unforgettable sunrise experience at Mount Bromo. At around 01:00 AM, you will begin your journey to witness the majestic night sky and the Milky Way over Bromo. After enjoying the stargazing, head to the main viewpoint to witness the sunrise over the volcanic landscape, where Mount Batok, Mount Bromo, and Gunung Semeru (Java’s highest and most active volcano) stand tall.
#next-stop#
Explore Mount Bromo & Crater Rim
Following sunrise, you will take an exciting 4WD ride or motorcycle journey across the Laotan Pasir (Sand Sea) to Poten, a Tenggerese Hindu temple at the base of Mount Bromo. From Poten, you can either take a 3km hike or opt for a pony ride to reach the base of Mount Bromo. After climbing 253 steps to the crater rim, you will be rewarded with spectacular views of the crater itself.
#next-stop#
Return to Surabaya
After enjoying the breathtaking views of Mount Bromo's crater, you will return to the hotel for breakfast and check-out. Finally, continue your journey to your hotel or Surabaya Airport, with an estimated travel time of around 4 hours. End of the program.

itinerary_days.title: A warm welcome to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity:
Pickup from Surabaya

Tour package bromo and ijen started with picked you up at Surabaya Airport your arrival time ( it will be better before 12 pm ) and then transfer out to hotel in Bondowoso (approx. 5 hrs) including stop at local restaurant along the way to having lunch with on own expenses.

itinerary_days.title: First stop: Ijen Crater
itinerary_days.activity:
Exploring the stunning Ijen Crater

journey begins at midnight with a drive to Paltuding, the gateway to the Ijen Crater. After a 1 to 1.5-hour trek, we’ll reach the crater rim, where the breathtaking turquoise sulfuric lake awaits. Witness the incredible efforts of sulfur miners braving the harsh conditions and marvel at the sunrise over this surreal volcanic landscape—a moment you won’t forget.


#next-stop#

Lunch and onward to Bromo

After descending, we’ll return to the hotel to freshen up, check out, and enjoy a hearty lunch. Then, we’ll embark on a scenic 3.5-hour drive to the Bromo area, where you’ll settle in for the night, ready for tomorrow’s adventures.

itinerary_days.title: From the Majestic Bromo Sunrise to the Hidden Madakaripura Waterfall
itinerary_days.activity:
Bromo: A sunrise like no other

Our adventure begins at 2:00 AM, as we head out to witness the dazzling Milky Way and the unforgettable sunrise over Mount Bromo. The caldera’s flat sea of volcanic sand gives way to the striking silhouettes of Mount Batok, the smoking Mount Bromo, and in the distance, the majestic Mount Semeru—the tallest and most active volcano in Java.

After soaking in the sunrise, we’ll ride across the iconic Sand Sea in a 4WD to Poten, a serene Tenggerese Hindu temple at the base of Mount Bromo. From here, you can either trek 3 km or opt for a pony ride to reach the foot of Mount Bromo. A short climb up 253 steps will bring you to the crater rim, where you’ll stand in awe of the volcano’s raw power and beauty.


#next-stop#

Madakaripura Waterfall and the journey back

Next, we drive an hour to the stunning Madakaripura Waterfall. A short 30-minute trek, guided by a local expert, leads us to this enchanting natural wonder hidden amidst towering cliffs. Don’t forget your slippers—you’ll be wading through shallow streams!

After the waterfall, we’ll begin the scenic 4-hour journey back to your hotel or the airport in Surabaya, marking the end of our unforgettable program.

(Please note: Ensure your flight departs after 7 PM on day 3.)

itinerary_days.title: Arrival in Surabaya and Journey to Bondowoso’s Retreat
itinerary_days.activity:
Arrival and Scenic Drive to Bondowoso
Begin your Bromo and Ijen adventure with a warm greeting at Surabaya Airport. After an early pick-up (best before noon), we’ll embark on a scenic 5-hour drive to Bondowoso, where East Java’s landscapes unfold before you. Upon arrival at hotel, take time to unwind and enjoy the tranquil surroundings. Indulge in a delicious dinner before resting up for the adventure ahead.

itinerary_days.title: Discovering Ijen Crater and the Bromo Area’s Mystical Charm
itinerary_days.activity:
Ijen Crater Expedition
At the break of midnight, our journey begins as we head towards Paltuding, the base of Ijen Crater. After a 90-minute drive, you’ll begin your 1 to 1.5-hour hike to the crater rim. There, you’ll be greeted by a surreal, turquoise sulfuric lake, surrounded by steep cliffs. Witness the strength of the local sulfur miners, who bravely navigate toxic fumes to carry heavy sacks of sulfur through the harsh terrain.
#next-stop#
Lunch and onward to Bromo
After descending, we’ll return to the hotel to freshen up, check out, and enjoy a hearty lunch. Then, we’ll embark on a scenic 3.5-hour drive to the Bromo area, where you’ll settle in for the night, ready for tomorrow’s adventures.

itinerary_days.title: Sunrise at Bromo and Exploration of Madakaripura Waterfall
itinerary_days.activity:
Bromo Sunrise and Stargazing
At 1:00 AM, we set off to witness the magical night sky at Bromo. The Milky Way will light up the volcanic landscape, setting the stage for an unforgettable sunrise. As the sun rises over the caldera, you’ll be in awe of the iconic views, with Mount Batok, Mount Bromo, and the mighty Mount Semeru standing tall against the horizon.
#next-stop#
Exploring the Volcanic Heart of Bromo
After sunrise, we journey across the vast Pasir (Sand Sea) in a 4WD vehicle, heading to Poten Temple. From here, you can choose to hike 3km or take a pony ride to the base of Mount Bromo. Climb the 253 steps to reach the crater rim, where you’ll witness the raw power of the active volcano up close.
#next-stop#
Madakaripura Waterfall: A Hidden Gem
Next, we drive to Madakaripura Waterfall, a one-hour journey through lush landscapes. A short trek of about 30 minutes (wear comfortable footwear!) will lead you to this stunning waterfall, nestled deep in the hills. After soaking in the beauty of the falls, we’ll continue our journey to Surabaya, where you’ll have the evening to unwind.
#next-stop#
Exploring Surabaya’s Vibrant G-Walk
Before heading back to your hotel, take some time to explore G-Walk, Surabaya’s popular hangout spot. Whether it’s shopping, tasting local delicacies, or enjoying the nightlife, this vibrant venue is the perfect place to relax after an adventurous day.
itinerary_days.hotel_name: Holiday Inn Express Surabaya Centerpoint, an IHG Hotel

itinerary_days.title: Farewell to East Java
itinerary_days.activity:
Departure
After a delicious breakfast at the hotel, you’ll be transferred to Juanda International Airport for your flight back home, with memories of your unforgettable adventure in East Java.

itinerary_days.title: Arrival in Surabaya and Journey to Bondowoso
itinerary_days.activity:
Arrival in Surabaya and Scenic Transfer to Bondowoso

Your exciting Bromo and Ijen adventure begins with a warm welcome at Surabaya Airport. We’ll pick you up (best before noon) and start our scenic 5-hour drive to Bondowoso. Along the way, we’ll stop at a local restaurant in Probolinggo for lunch at your own expense. Enjoy the changing landscapes as you make your way towards Bondowoso.


#next-stop#

Arrival at hotel

After arriving at hotel in Bondowoso, take some time to relax and settle in. Enjoy a delicious dinner and rest up for the next day’s adventure.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Crater Adventure and Papuma Beach Sunset
itinerary_days.activity: Ijen Crater Exploration

itinerary_days.title: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall and Journey to Bromo
itinerary_days.activity:
Tumpak Sewu Waterfall: The Thousand Waterfalls

Our journey begins early at 4:00 AM as we check out and head to Tumpak Sewu Waterfall, one of the most beautiful waterfalls in Java. Known for its unique half-circle formation, the waterfall consists of multiple mini-waterfalls cascading from a height of around 300 meters. A short, adventurous trek takes you across rocks and streams to reach the waterfall, so be sure to bring appropriate trekking sandals or shoes.


#next-stop#

Lunch and Journey to Bromo

After exploring the falls and perhaps even enjoying a natural water spa, we will have lunch before continuing our journey to the Bromo area. The drive will take approximately 4 hours, bringing you closer to the iconic Bromo Volcano.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise Adventure and Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity:
Bromo Stargazing and Sunrise

Prepare for an early morning adventure starting at 2:00 AM as we set out for stargazing and a Milky Way view at Mount Bromo. As the first rays of dawn break, witness the spectacular sunrise over the Bromo landscape. The majestic views of Mount Batok, Mount Bromo, and Mount Semeru will take your breath away, as they rise from the flat volcanic sand bed of the caldera.


#next-stop#

Exploring the Heart of Bromo

After sunrise, we will continue our journey on a thrilling 4WD ride over the vast Sand Sea to the Poten Temple. From here, you can either take a 3km hike or a pony ride to the base of Mount Bromo, where you will ascend 253 steps to reach the crater rim and witness the raw power of the volcano up close.


#next-stop#

Return to Surabaya

After a morning filled with adventure, we will head back to Surabaya. Depending on your travel plans, we recommend booking a flight or train departing after 6:00 PM. End your trip with wonderful memories of East Java’s volcanic wonders.

itinerary_days.title: Midnight Departure from Surabaya for Bromo Sunrise
itinerary_days.activity: This route leaves Surabaya late at night and heads directly toward the Bromo side for sunrise timing. After the jeep sequence, viewpoint access, crater handling, and the main Bromo stops, the route returns to Surabaya the same day.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to the Bromo Side for Overnight Staging
itinerary_days.activity: The first day is a clean transfer from Surabaya to the Bromo side so the sunrise sequence can be handled properly the next morning. The overnight stay reduces pressure compared with the faster one-day variant and gives the route a more stable opening.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity: The second day combines the Bromo sunrise sequence with the Madakaripura stop before returning to Surabaya. It remains a productive day, but the overnight staging makes the full route easier to read and more comfortable to execute.

itinerary_days.title: Welcome to Bromo - Surabaya to the Gateway of Bromo
itinerary_days.activity: Your adventure begins with a pickup from Surabaya Airport. Embark on a scenic 3-hour drive to the hotel in Cemoro Lawang, Bromo. After arrival, check-in at the Joglo Kecombrang Bromo for a restful evening. There are no meals included today, allowing you to relax and adjust to the beautiful surroundings.Your adventure begins with a pickup from Surabaya Airport. Embark on a scenic 3-hour drive to the hotel in Cemoro Lawang, Bromo. After arrival, check-in at the Joglo Kecombrang Bromo for a restful evening. There are no meals included today, allowing you to relax and adjust to the beautiful surroundings.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise – Exploring the Volcano and Waterfall Wonders
itinerary_days.activity: Your day begins at 1 AM with a preparation for stargazing and a view of the Milky Way over the Bromo caldera. Enjoy the surreal sunrise as it rises over the volcanic landscape, with Mount Batok, Mount Bromo, and the towering Semeru in the distance. The adventure continues with a thrilling 4WD ride or motorcycle ride over the Sand Sea to Poten Temple. From here, you can take a 3 km hike or a pony ride to reach the base of Mount Bromo and ascend 253 steps to get up-close views of the crater rim.After witnessing the incredible Bromo sunrise, we will journey to Madakaripura Waterfall. A 1-hour drive leads you to the entrance, followed by a 30-minute trek (wearing slippers) to this hidden gem of nature. After exploring, we’ll continue our journey to Bondowoso, with a stop at a local restaurant for lunch (at own expense). In the evening, check-in at hotel in Bondowoso for a well-deserved rest. Dinner is included at the hotel.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Crater – Sunrise Over a Sulfuric Landscape
itinerary_days.activity: Your Ijen adventure begins at midnight with a drive to the Ijen Crater parking area (Paltuding). From there, embark on a 1-1.5 hour trek to the crater rim, where you’ll be rewarded with views of the world-famous turquoise sulfuric lake and the rare blue fire phenomenon. Watch the brave sulfur miners at work, carrying heavy sacks of sulfur amidst the toxic fumes and challenging terrain.After soaking in the views of the Ijen Crater, return to the hotel for a shower and a light lunch. Then, begin your 4-hour drive back to Surabaya. Upon arrival, we will drop you off at your hotel or Surabaya Airport, concluding your adventure. Meals included: Breakfast and Lunch.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to the Bromo Side for Overnight Staging
itinerary_days.activity: The route begins with a transfer from Surabaya to the Bromo side so the sunrise sequence can be handled properly the next morning. This first day is about stable positioning rather than sightseeing overload.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Transfer Toward Ijen
itinerary_days.activity: After the Bromo sunrise sequence, the route continues through Madakaripura before transferring east toward the Ijen side. This is one of the heaviest connection days in the itinerary and works best when guests expect real movement across East Java.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Climb and Forward Finish into Bali
itinerary_days.activity: The final day completes the Ijen segment before the route continues across Ketapang and onward into Bali. It is a strong fit for travelers who want the East Java sequence to end with a clean forward handoff instead of a Surabaya return.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to Bondowoso for Ijen Staging
itinerary_days.activity: The first day is a dedicated transfer from Surabaya to the Bondowoso side so the route can stage properly for Ijen. After hotel check-in, the required health screening is completed before the climb window begins.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Climb and Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity: After the Ijen segment is completed, the route returns across East Java to Surabaya. This final day works best when guests treat the return as part of a serious two-day Ijen sequence, not as an afterthought.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to Bondowoso for Ijen Staging
itinerary_days.activity: The route begins with a transfer from Surabaya to the Bondowoso side so the Ijen segment can be handled properly. After hotel check-in, the required screening is completed before the climb window begins.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Climb and Transfer Toward the Bromo Side
itinerary_days.activity: After the Ijen sequence, the route moves west toward the Bromo side. This day is the operational bridge of the package and works best when guests understand it as a serious transfer day, not a light scenic drive.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Return to Surabaya
itinerary_days.activity: The final day combines the Bromo sunrise sequence with the Madakaripura stop before the route returns to Surabaya. It is productive and high-contrast, but still depends on disciplined timing across the full day.

itinerary_days.title: Surabaya to Bondowoso for Ijen Staging
itinerary_days.activity: The first day positions the route on the Bondowoso side so the Ijen climb can be handled properly the next morning. Screening is completed after hotel check-in.

itinerary_days.title: Ijen Climb and Transfer Toward the Bromo Side
itinerary_days.activity: After the Ijen sequence, the route continues west toward the Bromo side. This remains one of the heavier operational days, but the wider itinerary gives more room around it.

itinerary_days.title: Bromo Sunrise, Madakaripura, and Return to Surabaya City
itinerary_days.activity: The main route sequence finishes with Bromo and Madakaripura before returning to Surabaya for the final overnight. This structure reduces departure-day pressure compared with the tighter 3-day loop.

knowledge_bases.title: Hotel and Accommodation Details
knowledge_bases.content: Packages include accommodation in quality hotels. Rooms are typically shared double occupancy with a private bathroom. Twin beds can be provided on request. Upgraded hotel or extended stays are possible for an extra cost.

knowledge_bases.title: Recommended Arrival and Departure Times
knowledge_bases.content: For a smooth start, the latest possible pick-up time on Day 1 is 16:00 (4:00 PM). For your departure, we recommend booking a flight that leaves at 18:00 (6:00 PM) or later on the last day of the tour. A departure time of 20:00 (8:00 PM) is considered even safer to allow for contingencies like traffic.

knowledge_bases.title: Best Time to Visit
knowledge_bases.content: The best time to visit for weather is typically during the dry season, which runs from April to October. The rainy season is generally around November - March.

knowledge_bases.title: Mount Bromo Tour Details
knowledge_bases.content: The tour features a sunrise tour via a private 4WD jeep. Weather permitting, you can also view the Milky Way. An optional horse ride is available. Your main luggage will remain safely at the hotel during the sunrise tour.

knowledge_bases.title: Cancellation and Refund Policy
knowledge_bases.content: A full refund is provided for cancellations made 48 hours or more before the tour start time. For cancellations made less than 48 hours before the tour, a 50% fee is charged. This is because non-refundable costs for services like accommodation and transport have already been secured. Additionally, there is no refund for any attractions or activities that are missed or skipped by a guest during the tour.

knowledge_bases.title: Complimentary Custom Travel T-Shirt
knowledge_bases.content: Yes, a high-quality black T-shirt is included for every tour participant. A range of sizes is available, and customization with a logo or name is possible upon request.

knowledge_bases.title: Contacting Customer Support
knowledge_bases.content:
Our customer support team is available to assist you daily from 08:00 to 22:00 WIB (GMT+7). You can reach us through:
- Email: info@javavolcano-touroperator.com
- WhatsApp: +62 822-4478-8833

knowledge_bases.title: Crew Allocation (Driver and Guide)
knowledge_bases.content:
Our crew is allocated based on group size for the best experience:
- or groups of 1–3 people: Your driver will also serve as your guide, and you will have local guides at each destination.
- For groups of 4 or more people: You will have a dedicated driver and an escort guide accompanying you throughout the tour.

knowledge_bases.title: Meeting Your Driver
knowledge_bases.content: Your driver's name and WhatsApp contact number will be sent to you one day before the tour begins. The driver will be waiting for you at your designated pick-up point holding a signboard with your name on it for easy identification.

knowledge_bases.title: Estimated Travel Times
knowledge_bases.content:
Here are some estimated travel times between key locations:
- Surabaya to Bromo: ~3 hours
- Tumpak Sewu to Bromo: 3-4 hours
- Ijen to Surabaya: 5-6 hours
- Bali to Ijen: 5-7 hours

knowledge_bases.title: Summary of Tour Exclusions
knowledge_bases.content:
The following are typically not included in the tour price:
- Meals that are not specified in the itinerary.
- Optional add-ons like horse riding, professional photography, water shoes, gloves, and travel insurance.
- Personal expenses such as snacks, souvenirs, and tips.
- Train tickets (e.g., Surabaya-Yogyakarta).

knowledge_bases.title: Group Benefits and FOC Policy
knowledge_bases.content:
We offer "Free of Charge" (FOC) benefits for larger group bookings:
- 18+ paying guests: 1 person travels free (1 FOC).
- 35+ paying guests: 2 people travel free (2 FOC).
- 50+ paying guests: 3 people travel free, plus the entire group receives a 5% discount.

knowledge_bases.title: Booking Process
knowledge_bases.content: To ensure your reservation is securely and accurately registered, all initial bookings must be made directly through the official JVTO website. This process guarantees that all your details are captured correctly, which is essential for coordinating logistics. Once your initial booking is complete, our team can assist you with any further adjustments or add-ons.

knowledge_bases.title: Ijen Crater Health Certificate Requirement
knowledge_bases.content: A medical health certificate is required to trek Mount Ijen as per government regulations. This medical check-up is already included in your tour package, and our team will arrange a nurse to visit your hotel to perform the health screening evening before the hike.

knowledge_bases.title: Contingency Plan if Unfit for Ijen Trek
knowledge_bases.content: If a guest is declared unfit to climb Ijen after the health check, we will politely explain it is for their safety per government regulations. A refund for the Ijen portion is not possible as costs have been allocated, but we will offer the guest the option to rest comfortably at the hotel while the group continues the hike.

knowledge_bases.title: Ijen Crater Tour Details
knowledge_bases.content: The Ijen tour features a midnight trek to see the famous "blue fire." The trek is of moderate difficulty, taking about 1.5-2 hours each way. For those who need assistance, a "trolley ojek" (man-powered cart) is available at an extra cost. The crater is closed to visitors on the first Friday of every month.

knowledge_bases.title: Summary of Tour Inclusions
knowledge_bases.content:
Our all-inclusive packages typically cover:
- Transport: A private air-conditioned vehicle, including fuel, tolls, and parking fees.
- Accommodation: Quality hotels with breakfast included.
- Guides: An English-speaking local guide and/or driver.
- Activities: A private 4WD jeep for the Bromo sunrise tour and all entrance fees and permits.
- Gear: Gas masks, trekking poles.
- Food & Drink: Mineral water and selected meals as specified in the itinerary.
- Extras: A custom travel T-shirt, ferry tickets (for packages ends in Bali), and full assistance during the trip.

knowledge_bases.title: JVTO Licensing and Legitimacy
knowledge_bases.content: Yes, Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO) is a fully legal and officially licensed tourism company registered with the Indonesian government, operating since 2016. We have a physical office in Bondowoso, East Java, and are fully compliant with all local tourism regulations.

knowledge_bases.title: Advice on Money Exchange
knowledge_bases.content: It is recommended to exchange currency in downtown areas rather than at airports, as the rates are typically better. This is merely advice; JVTO is not affiliated with any money changers.

knowledge_bases.title: Recommended Packing List
knowledge_bases.content: We recommend packing layered clothing, a waterproof jacket, hiking shoes, and water shoes (for waterfalls). It is also useful to bring a power bank and any personal medications you may need.

knowledge_bases.title: Papuma Beach Details
knowledge_bases.content: Papuma Beach is a coastal destination perfect for viewing the sunrise or sunset. No special Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) is required to visit.

knowledge_bases.title: Partial Tours or Transport-Only Services
knowledge_bases.content: JVTO does not offer unbundled services such as "transport-only" or partially customized tours without accommodation. This policy is in place to maintain end-to-end quality control over your entire experience. By managing all key components, we can ensure a consistent standard of service and a seamless, coordinated adventure.

knowledge_bases.title: Accepted Payment Methods and Deadlines
knowledge_bases.content:
We accept payment via cash, bank transfer, or other Wise-approved channels. Final payment deadlines vary by method to ensure funds clear before the tour starts:
- Credit/Debit Cards: At least 5 days before your tour date.
- Bank Transfers / Wise: At least 3 days before your tour date.
- Cash on Arrival: This option may be possible but must be pre-approved.

knowledge_bases.title: Payment Terms and Deposit
knowledge_bases.content: A 20% deposit of the total tour cost is required to confirm your booking. The remaining 80% balance is due before your tour begins. For any tour booked less than 14 days before the departure time, full (100%) upfront payment is required to secure the reservation.

knowledge_bases.title: Pick-up and Drop-off Locations
knowledge_bases.content: We offer flexible pick-up and drop-off locations to suit your travel plans, including Surabaya (airport, hotel, train station), Malang city, and Bali (your accommodation or Ketapang Harbour).

knowledge_bases.title: Pick-up Procedure at Airport, Hotel, or Train Station
knowledge_bases.content: Our team will be waiting for you at your designated pick-up point, whether it's at the airport, hotel, or train station. The driver will hold a signboard displaying your name for easy identification. This ensures a smooth and stress-free start to your tour.

knowledge_bases.title: Price Negotiation and Discounts
knowledge_bases.content: JVTO's tour prices are fixed and not negotiable. The prices are carefully calculated to reflect the high quality of services and comprehensive inclusions. For larger groups, we offer special "Free of Charge" (FOC) benefits.

knowledge_bases.title: Rescheduling Policy
knowledge_bases.content: Rescheduling your tour is possible but is subject to the availability of our crew and accommodations, and may be subject to potential fees. For unforeseen circumstances like flight cancellations, we can provide an official letter to support your travel insurance claim.

knowledge_bases.title: JVTO Safety Record and Protocols
knowledge_bases.content: JVTO has a 100% safety record. Our team consists of dedicated and respectful local crew who provide extra care for all guests, including children. Immediate assistance is provided in case of a medical issue.

knowledge_bases.title: Can You Accommodate Special Dietary Needs?
knowledge_bases.content: Yes, we cater to various dietary requirements including vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Please inform us of your dietary needs when booking so we can make the necessary arrangements in advance.

knowledge_bases.title: Special Services and Requests
knowledge_bases.content: Yes, upon request, the company can arrange for special services such as photo events and make special arrangements for groups. Drone usage is also permitted but should be operated safely and in accordance with local regulations.

knowledge_bases.title: Temperature and Weather at Mount Bromo and Mount Ijen
knowledge_bases.content:
Temperatures at Mount Bromo and Mount Ijen can be quite cold, especially during sunrise. On average:
- Mount Bromo: 5–15°C (41–59°F)
- Mount Ijen: 10–15°C (50–59°F)
We recommend bringing warm clothing such as jackets, gloves, and scarves to ensure comfort during early-morning activities.

knowledge_bases.title: Itinerary Customization Policy ("Micro-customization")
knowledge_bases.content: We allow "micro-customizations" that do not significantly alter the primary route or duration. Approved examples include: requesting twin beds instead of a double bed, upgrading your hotel room category (may incur extra costs), adding optional on-site activities like horse riding, or adjusting meal times if the schedule permits. However, major deviations like visiting a city far from the planned route or changing the fundamental order of destinations (e.g., Ijen before Bromo) are not accommodated to maintain the tour's quality and schedule.

knowledge_bases.title: Types of Tours Offered (Private, All-Inclusive)
knowledge_bases.content: All JVTO tours are exclusively private, "all-inclusive" experiences, part of a "curated experience" model designed for maximum quality and comfort. We do not offer shared or open-group tours. This private model ensures comprehensive end-to-end quality control, maximum flexibility, and a personalized journey. By managing all trip components internally—transportation, accommodation, entrance fees, guides—we guarantee a consistent, high standard of service.

knowledge_bases.title: Train Tickets to/from Yogyakarta
knowledge_bases.content: Train tickets, for example for travel between Surabaya and Yogyakarta, are not included in tour packages. Guests are responsible for booking their own train tickets.

knowledge_bases.title: Travel Insurance
knowledge_bases.content: Travel insurance is not included in our packages. While it is optional, we highly recommend that all travelers purchase their own comprehensive travel insurance for personal protection against unforeseen events.

knowledge_bases.title: Vehicle Types Used
knowledge_bases.content:
We use private, air-conditioned vehicles suited to your group's size:
- 1-3 passengers: An MPV (e.g., Toyota Avanza).
- 4-11 passengers: A Toyota Hiace Van.
- More than 11 passengers: Additional vehicles will be used.

knowledge_bases.title: Waterfall Treks (Tumpak Sewu & Madakaripura)
knowledge_bases.content: The treks to Tumpak Sewu and Madakaripura waterfalls are beautiful but can be challenging, as the paths are steep and wet. Helmets are provided for safety at both locations. It is highly recommended to wear water shoes for better grip.
